title: China’s New Investment Regulations Block Strategic Technology Transfer
gist: China's State Council Decree No. 837, effective July 1, 2026, tightens Beijing's control over what technology, personnel, and know-how Chinese companies can transfer when investing abroad, completing a regulatory trio with Decree 834 (supply chain security) and Decree 835 (countering foreign extraterritorial jurisdiction) that together give the state sweeping levers over both inbound and outbound strategic technology flows.

insights.claim: China's State Council Decree No. 837, entering into force July 1, 2026, requires Chinese companies investing abroad to submit to closer state control over which technology they can transfer, who they can send to implement it, and how much know-how they can share.
insights.speaker: María Herrero Martínez
insights.so_what: Directly constrains Chinese outbound FDI in tech-intensive sectors, relevant for evaluating Chinese tech joint ventures and licensing deals abroad going forward.
insights.locator: China's State Council Decree No. 837 enters into force on July 1

insights.claim: Decree No. 837 received less international attention than the two decrees that preceded it because it only affects Chinese companies rather than targeting foreign states or firms directly.
insights.speaker: María Herrero Martínez
insights.so_what: The muted global attention on Decree 837 despite its significance suggests investors may be underpricing how much it will constrain Chinese firms' overseas tech deals compared to the more visible 834/835.
insights.locator: Since it only affects Chinese companies

themes.theme: Part of a broader regulatory trio

insights.claim: Decree No. 834 treats industrial and supply chain security as a national security matter, giving Chinese authorities a legal basis to intervene in supply chains, while Decree No. 835 authorizes countermeasures against foreign companies or individuals over what China deems improper extraterritorial jurisdiction.
insights.speaker: María Herrero Martínez
insights.so_what: Together with Decree 837, Beijing now has a coordinated regulatory toolkit spanning inbound supply-chain security, outbound retaliation powers, and outbound tech-transfer control, a fuller picture of China's economic-security legal architecture for investors assessing China exposure.
insights.locator: Decree No. 834 on supply chain security
